source_textbook.name: Khan Academy Mathematics 6th-8th Grade
source_textbook.section: Ratios, Expressions, Functions, Pythagorean Theorem, Statistics

extracted_concepts.name: ratio
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Comparison of two quantities by division, written as a:b or a/b

extracted_concepts.name: unit rate
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A rate with denominator of 1

extracted_concepts.name: proportional relationship
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A relationship where y = kx (constant ratio)

extracted_concepts.name: constant of proportionality
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The constant k in y = kx

extracted_concepts.name: greatest common factor
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Largest number that divides two or more numbers evenly

extracted_concepts.name: least common multiple
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Smallest number that is a multiple of two or more numbers

extracted_concepts.name: integer
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Whole numbers including negatives: ..., -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3, ...

extracted_concepts.name: rational number
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A number that can be written as a fraction a/b where b ≠ 0

extracted_concepts.name: irrational number
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A number that cannot be written as a fraction (e.g., π, √2)

extracted_concepts.name: exponent
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The power to which a number is raised: 2³ = 8

extracted_concepts.name: square root
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A number that when multiplied by itself gives the original number

extracted_concepts.name: numerical expression
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A mathematical phrase with numbers and operations

extracted_concepts.name: algebraic expression
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A mathematical phrase with numbers, operations, and variables

extracted_concepts.name: equation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A statement that two expressions are equal

extracted_concepts.name: inequality
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A statement comparing two expressions using <, >, ≤, ≥

extracted_concepts.name: slope
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Rate of change of a linear function (rise/run)

extracted_concepts.name: y-intercept
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: The y-value where a line crosses the y-axis

extracted_concepts.name: linear function
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A function of the form y = mx + b

extracted_concepts.name: function
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A rule that assigns each input exactly one output

extracted_concepts.name: Pythagorean theorem
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: In a right triangle: a² + b² = c²

extracted_concepts.name: congruent figures
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Figures that are the same shape and size

extracted_concepts.name: similar figures
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Figures with the same shape but different sizes

extracted_concepts.name: dilation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A transformation that changes size but not shape

extracted_concepts.name: scatter plot
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A graph showing the relationship between two variables

extracted_concepts.name: line of best fit
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A line that best represents data on a scatter plot

extracted_concepts.name: two-way table
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A table showing frequencies for two categorical variables

extracted_concepts.name: complementary angles
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Two angles that sum to 90 degrees

extracted_concepts.name: supplementary angles
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Two angles that sum to 180 degrees

extracted_concepts.name: vertical angles
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Opposite angles formed by intersecting lines (equal)

extracted_concepts.name: translation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Sliding a figure in a straight line

extracted_concepts.name: rotation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Turning a figure around a point

extracted_concepts.name: reflection
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Flipping a figure over a line

extracted_concepts.name: probability
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: A measure of how likely an event is to occur (0 to 1)

extracted_concepts.name: mean absolute deviation
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Average distance of data points from the mean

extracted_concepts.name: interquartile range
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Difference between Q3 and Q1, measuring spread

extracted_concepts.name: compound interest
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Interest calculated on principal plus accumulated interest

extracted_concepts.name: simple interest
extracted_concepts.definition_snippet: Interest calculated only on the principal

extracted_relations.evidence: Unit rate is a specific type of ratio
Proportional relationships maintain constant ratio
The constant k defines the proportional relationship
GCF is the largest common factor
LCM is the smallest common multiple
Rational numbers can be written as fractions
Irrational numbers are the complement of rational numbers
Exponents represent repeated multiplication
Square root is the inverse of squaring
Algebraic expressions include variables
An equation contains two expressions
Inequalities compare expressions instead of equating
Slope is the rate of change in a linear function
y-intercept is where the line crosses the y-axis
Linear functions are a type of function
Pythagorean theorem applies to right triangles
Congruent figures are similar with ratio 1
Dilation produces similar figures
Slope is explained using similar triangles
Probability is a ratio of favorable to total outcomes
Compound interest uses exponential growth
Simple interest uses multiplication
